pick_device.title: Select your Pixoo 64
pick_device.description: These devices were found on your network via Divoom's discovery service. Pick one, or enter an IP address manually.

manual.title: Connect to your Pixoo 64
manual.description: Enter the local IP address of your Divoom Pixoo 64. A test connection (Channel/GetAllConf) is made before the entry is created.

error.cannot_connect: Cannot connect to the device. Check the IP address and that it's on the same network.

init.title: Configure your Pixoo device
init.description: Update the device IP, the default page duration, and its pages. Pages are a raw YAML list of named entries. By default each page has a `components` list, but a page can instead set a `page_type` (clock, channel, visualizer, sound_meter, pv, fuel) for a native Pixoo screen or a prebuilt layout - see the README. The default duration applies to any page that doesn't set its own `duration`. Other optional per-page fields: `enabled` (Jinja template), `scan_interval` (seconds between refreshes while shown).

error.cannot_connect: Cannot connect to the device. Check the IP address and that it's on the same network.
error.invalid_schema: Each page needs a `name` (text) field, and either a `components` (list) field or, for page_type clock/channel/visualizer, an `id` field.

render_page.name: Render page
render_page.description: Composes a page (a list of components, or a page configured in the integration's options) onto the Pixoo 64 and pushes it to the screen.

device_id.name: Device
device_id.description: The Pixoo Canvas device to render on.

page.name: Page name
page.description: Name of a page configured in the integration's options, of any page_type (components, clock, channel, visualizer, sound_meter, pv, fuel, pihole, weather, battery). Provide this, or an inline page (page_type/components/id/etc.), not both.

page_type.name: Page type
page_type.description: Only used for an inline page (omit when using 'page'). One of components (the default), clock, channel, visualizer, sound_meter, pv, fuel, pihole, weather, battery. clock/channel/visualizer also need an 'id'; sound_meter takes no extra field; pv/fuel/pihole/weather/battery take their own fields (power/storage/discharge/time for pv; title/name1/price1/name2/price2/name3/price3/status for fuel; blocked/percentage/queries/status_entity for pihole; entity for weather, reading condition/temperature/humidity from that one weather entity; entity/label for battery, a generic 0-100 charge gauge for any entity) passed as extra service data alongside page_type. See the README for field details.

components.name: Components
components.description: Inline list of page components (text/image/rectangle/icon/progress_bar/templatable). Text components draw static buffer text by default, with an optional `font` (pico_8, the default bitmap font; gicko, wider; matrix_chunky_6/matrix_chunky_8, with real lowercase and French accents); with `scroll: true` they instead queue a native, device-animated scrolling overlay (Draw/SendHttpText) rather than static buffer text. Used for an inline page_type: components page (the default page_type); provide this or 'page', not both.

id.name: Id
id.description: Native screen/channel index to switch to. Required when page_type is clock, channel, or visualizer. Accepts a number or a Jinja template.

power.name: Power
power.description: Solar power, e.g. "1200" (rendered with a "W" suffix). Used when page_type is pv.

storage.name: Storage
storage.description: Battery charge percentage (0-100). Used when page_type is pv.

discharge.name: Discharge
discharge.description: Optional discharge power, e.g. "300" (rendered with a "W" suffix). Omit to hide the discharge readout. Used when page_type is pv.

time.name: Time
time.description: Optional time label; defaults to the current HH:MM if omitted. Used when page_type is pv.

status.name: Status
status.description: Optional status line. Used when page_type is fuel.

blocked.name: Blocked
blocked.description: Ads blocked today. Used when page_type is pihole.

percentage.name: Percentage
percentage.description: Blocking rate, 0-100. Used when page_type is pihole.

queries.name: Queries
queries.description: Optional DNS queries today. Used when page_type is pihole.

status_entity.name: Status entity
status_entity.description: Optional binary_sensor reflecting whether Pi-hole is active, used to switch the on/off shield icon+color. Used when page_type is pihole.

entity.name: Entity
entity.description: Required for both page_type weather (a weather.* entity - its condition/temperature/humidity are read directly from that entity) and page_type battery (any entity whose state is a 0-100 charge percentage).

label.name: Label
label.description: Optional short name shown under the gauge, e.g. "Robot". Used when page_type is battery.

variables.name: Variables
variables.description: Extra variables made available to the page's Jinja templates.

play_buzzer.name: Play the buzzer
play_buzzer.description: Plays the Pixoo's built-in buzzer. Use at your own risk - repeated/rapid use of the buzzer could stress the device's hardware over time.

device_id.name: Device
device_id.description: The Pixoo Canvas device to buzz.

active_time_ms.name: Buzz time per cycle
active_time_ms.description: How long the buzzer is on for each cycle.

off_time_ms.name: Idle time per cycle
off_time_ms.description: How long the buzzer is off for between cycles.

total_time_ms.name: Total time
total_time_ms.description: Total duration the buzzer keeps cycling on/off.

reboot_device.name: Reboot the device
reboot_device.description: Reboots the Pixoo (Device/SysReboot). The screen goes dark for a while and any active rotation resumes once the device comes back online. Use for recovery automations (e.g. after a device becomes unresponsive), not as a routine action.

device_id.name: Device
device_id.description: The Pixoo Canvas device to reboot.

start_timer.name: Start the countdown timer
start_timer.description: Starts the Pixoo's built-in countdown timer tool (Tools/SetTimer), switching the screen to it. It takes over the screen until stop_timer is called or another page/service switches the device away from it - handy to call from an iOS/Android Shortcut (Home Assistant's app exposes any service as a "Perform action" step) for a quick kitchen timer.

device_id.name: Device
device_id.description: The Pixoo Canvas device to start the timer on.

minutes.name: Minutes
minutes.description: Countdown minutes (0-59). At least one of minutes/seconds must be non-zero.

seconds.name: Seconds
seconds.description: Countdown seconds (0-59). At least one of minutes/seconds must be non-zero.

stop_timer.name: Stop the countdown timer
stop_timer.description: Stops the Pixoo's built-in countdown timer tool, if running, restores the channel that was on screen before start_timer (Channel/SetIndex, for a clean exit instead of leaving the timer's own frame on screen), and resumes page rotation if start_timer had paused it. There's no pause_timer: confirmed on real hardware (and in Divoom's own app) that stopping a countdown timer always resets it to 0, so a temporary pause that resumes mid-countdown isn't possible.

device_id.name: Device
device_id.description: The Pixoo Canvas device to stop the timer on.

start_stopwatch.name: Start the stopwatch
start_stopwatch.description: Starts the Pixoo's built-in stopwatch tool (Tools/SetStopWatch), switching the screen to it. It takes over the screen until stop_stopwatch is called or another page/service switches the device away from it - handy to call from an iOS/Android Shortcut for timing something on the fly.

device_id.name: Device
device_id.description: The Pixoo Canvas device to start the stopwatch on.

stop_stopwatch.name: Stop the stopwatch
stop_stopwatch.description: Stops the Pixoo's built-in stopwatch tool, if running, restores the channel that was on screen before start_stopwatch (Channel/SetIndex, for a clean exit instead of leaving the stopwatch's own frame on screen), and resumes page rotation if start_stopwatch had paused it - use this when you're done with the stopwatch. For a temporary pause you intend to resume shortly (without page rotation kicking back in, and keeping the elapsed time on screen), use pause_stopwatch instead.

device_id.name: Device
device_id.description: The Pixoo Canvas device to stop the stopwatch on.

pause_stopwatch.name: Pause the stopwatch
pause_stopwatch.description: Pauses the Pixoo's built-in stopwatch tool without restoring the channel active before start_stopwatch or resuming page rotation, keeping the elapsed time on screen so start_stopwatch can resume it later. Same underlying command as stop_stopwatch, minus the channel restore and page rotation resume - use stop_stopwatch instead when you're done with the stopwatch for good.

device_id.name: Device
device_id.description: The Pixoo Canvas device to pause the stopwatch on.

reset_stopwatch.name: Reset the stopwatch
reset_stopwatch.description: Resets the Pixoo's built-in stopwatch back to zero (Tools/SetStopWatch, Status: 2). Whether this also stops it if it was running is untested on real hardware - call stop_stopwatch afterwards if it doesn't.

device_id.name: Device
device_id.description: The Pixoo Canvas device to reset the stopwatch on.

start_visualizer.name: Start the visualizer
start_visualizer.description: Switches the Pixoo to one of its built-in audio visualizers (Channel/SetEqPosition), pausing page rotation first if it's running - handy to call from an iOS/Android Shortcut without building an inline render_page page. Call stop_visualizer to return to whatever was on screen before.

device_id.name: Device
device_id.description: The Pixoo Canvas device to switch to the visualizer.

id.name: Id
id.description: Index of the visualizer, as shown in the Divoom app (starting at 0).

stop_visualizer.name: Stop the visualizer
stop_visualizer.description: Restores the channel that was active before start_visualizer, and resumes page rotation if start_visualizer had paused it.

device_id.name: Device
device_id.description: The Pixoo Canvas device to stop the visualizer on.

start_sound_meter.name: Start the sound meter
start_sound_meter.description: Switches the Pixoo to its built-in sound meter (decibel) tool (Tools/SetNoiseStatus), pausing page rotation first if it's running - handy to call from an iOS/Android Shortcut. Call stop_sound_meter to return to whatever was on screen before.

device_id.name: Device
device_id.description: The Pixoo Canvas device to start the sound meter on.

stop_sound_meter.name: Stop the sound meter
stop_sound_meter.description: Stops the sound meter tool, restores the channel that was active before start_sound_meter (Channel/SetIndex, for a clean exit instead of leaving the sound meter's own frame on screen), and resumes page rotation if start_sound_meter had paused it.

device_id.name: Device
device_id.description: The Pixoo Canvas device to stop the sound meter on.

set_alarm.name: Set the alarm
set_alarm.description: Sets the Pixoo's built-in alarm clock (Device/SetAlarm) to ring at the given time. Unlike start_timer/start_stopwatch/start_visualizer, this doesn't take over the screen immediately and doesn't need page rotation paused - it's a scheduled wake handled entirely by the device's own firmware.

device_id.name: Device
device_id.description: The Pixoo Canvas device to set the alarm on.

time.name: Time
time.description: Alarm time (24-hour). Only the hour and minute are used.

enabled.name: Enabled
enabled.description: Whether the alarm is enabled. Defaults to true.

stop_alarm.name: Stop the alarm
stop_alarm.description: Disables the Pixoo's built-in alarm clock (Device/SetAlarm, Status: 0).

device_id.name: Device
device_id.description: The Pixoo Canvas device to disable the alarm on.
